title: Segmentation fault (core dumped) when combining multiple channels

posts.name: Alexandros Attikis
posts.text:
Dear experts,
I am trying to combine multiple regions (~80 over all Run 2 eras) and when I run combine on the resultant cards i get the following error:
Segmentation fault (core dumped)
I have attached the full log for 1 mass point as an example, along with the datacard used as input.
The problem does not seem to happen with a subset of 20-30 categories (e.g. categories within same Run era2). Is there a limitation with CPU resources or can the problem lie elsewhere? (e.g. datacards).
Greatly appreciate some help narrowing down the problem.
Best regards,
Alexandros
Archive.zip (1.2 MB)
i
posts.cooked_html:
Dear experts,

I am trying to combine multiple regions (~80 over all Run 2 eras) and when I run combine on the resultant cards i get the following error:
Segmentation fault (core dumped)
I have attached the full log for 1 mass point as an example, along with the datacard used as input.

The problem does not seem to happen with a subset of 20-30 categories (e.g. categories within same Run era2). Is there a limitation with CPU resources or can the problem lie elsewhere? (e.g. datacards).

Greatly appreciate some help narrowing down the problem.

Best regards,
Alexandros

Archive.zip (1.2 MB)
i

posts.name: Philip Keicher
posts.text:
Hi Alexandros,
have you tried setting
ulimit -s unlimited

before running anything? It’s a known issue for large combinations that the default user limits are too low.
Cheers,
Philip
posts.cooked_html:
Hi Alexandros,

have you tried setting

ulimit -s unlimited

before running anything? It’s a known issue for large combinations that the default user limits are too low.

Cheers,
Philip

posts.name: Alexandros Attikis
posts.text:
Hi Philip,
that did the trick indeed (albeit with the tcsh variation “limit stacksize unlimited”)!
Thank you for your help.
Cheers,
Alexandros
posts.cooked_html:
Hi Philip,

Alexandros
